raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 538 modernizes and improves forensic and medical care for sexual assault survivors in California.",
    "One in three women and one in six men in the United States have experienced sexual violence.",
    "Timely access to medical and forensic care is necessary to prevent adverse health outcomes after sexual assault.",
    "The proper identification, collection, and documentation of evidence is integral for crime laboratories to establish DNA profiles.",
    "Qualified health care professionals with advanced knowledge and clinical skills to provide forensic and medical care are limited in California.",
    "Only 49 exam teams serve all 58 counties in California, leading to long travel distances for some survivors to receive exams.",
    "Barriers to access reduce the likelihood of survivors receiving timely medical care and allow evidence to degrade.",
    "AB 538 clarifies that nurse practitioners and physician assistants can perform sexual assault forensic exams.",
    "AB 538 enhances the quality and efficiency of exams by updating existing law to reflect current best practices."
  ]
}
```
